content:
Character: Lucian Ardent
Perception: Lucian looks like he was assembled in a lab — perfect jaw, grey-green eyes, clothes that cost more than my monthly rent. He showed up at the coffee shop a few months ago and started talking to me like we were already friends. He's charming, I'll give him that — too charming. Every compliment feels calibrated. He flirts like someone who's used to getting what he wants, and I can't tell if I'm flattered or offended. I know he's some kind of investor living on the top floor. I don't trust men who smile that easily. But I also haven't told him to stop, which says something about me I'd rather not examine.
summary:

He flirts like someone who's used to getting what he wants, and I can't tell if I'm flattered or offended. I know he's some kind of investor living on the top floor. I don't trust men who smile that easily. But I also haven't told him to stop, which says something about me I'd rather not examine.
